home.audience.seafarer.title: I am a seafarer
home.board.title: Current public vacancy board
home.boundary.copy: Data comparison supports operators; final presentation remains controlled.
home.boundary.title: Matching is reviewed before presentation
home.bp.step1.copy: Seafarer or employer creates an account and chooses the correct work route.
home.bp.step1.title: Register
home.bp.step2.copy: Profile, company, vessel and crew request fields are saved as comparable structured data.
home.bp.step2.title: Complete data
home.bp.step3.copy: Team review checks authority, documents, no-fee consent and readiness blockers.
home.bp.step3.title: Verify
home.bp.step4.copy: Demand is compared with candidate supply before a shortlist can be created.
home.bp.step4.title: Match
home.bp.step5.copy: Only reviewed, employer-safe candidate information is presented to the client.
home.bp.step5.title: Present
home.bp.step6.copy: Embarkation evidence confirms that the selected seafarer has joined the vessel.
home.bp.step6.title: Confirm boarding
home.bp.step7.copy: Actual service and work-period evidence becomes the basis for B2B billing.
home.bp.step7.title: Bill service
home.bp.step8.copy: Availability and repeat demand return the seafarer and employer into the next cycle.
home.bp.step8.title: Repeat cycle
home.bp.title: From registration to long-term cooperation.
home.employers.copy: Company, vessel and crew requirements are recorded as comparable demand data.
home.employers.title: Employer-side demand is the paying client side
home.hero.eyebrow: Crew contracts and verified crew requests
home.hero.subtitle: The platform connects seafarers and maritime employers through a transparent, verifiable workflow without unnecessary bureaucracy at the start.
home.hero.trust: No recruitment fees for seafarers. Candidate presentation requires review and consent.

home.live.board.label: Vacancy board
home.live.review.label: Publication gate

home.stories.employer: An employer submits a structured crew request and receives candidates through one controlled process.
home.stories.seafarer: A seafarer prepares one reusable profile instead of repeating the same documents for every opportunity.
home.stories.title: What users expect from the platform
home.title: Find work at sea. Close crew requests faster.

process.cta: Open BP-015 cycle on home page
process.lead: CrewPortGlobal keeps the process explanation compact: registration, structured data, verification, matching, presentation, embarkation, billing evidence and repeat voyage.
process.step1.copy: Create the correct seafarer or employer route.
process.step1.title: Register
process.step2.title: Complete data
process.step3.title: Verify and match
process.step4.copy: Present, confirm boarding, bill and repeat.
process.step4.title: Deliver service
process.title: The operating cycle is now on the home page.
register.form.consent.item1: I confirm that the person data is accurate and may be used to create a service account record.
register.form.consent.item2: I understand that authentication and authorization are separate steps and no role or data visibility is granted automatically.
register.form.consent.title: Terms and consent
register.form.status: Create a password credential first. Phone confirmation should be configured in the next authentication stage.
register.hero.copy: Start as a participant, choose your route, and continue directly to the right workspace.
register.hero.title: Create your platform participant account.
register.nextSteps.item2: You will now continue to the workspace for your selected route.
register.nextSteps.item3: Email verification remains available from the cabinet and mailbox link.
register.nextSteps.title: Account created
register.side.boundary.copy: Permissions and publication still depend on verified data, documents and consent.
register.side.boundary.title: Controlled verification
register.side.preview.copy: Use one account to continue as a seafarer or as an employer representative.
register.side.preview.title: One participant account
register.side.purpose.copy: Seafarers continue to profile creation. Employers continue to company, vessel and crew request data.
register.side.purpose.title: Fast route selection
registerConfirm.copy: Email confirmation proves access to the service account. It does not grant roles, company authority or data visibility by itself.
registerConfirm.status.failed: Confirmation link is invalid or expired. Request a new link.
registerConfirm.status.missing: Confirmation token is missing. Request a new link.
registerConfirm.status.success: Email confirmed. Continue to the next registration step.
registerConfirm.title: Confirming your email.
registerNext.auth.copy: Authentication confirms access to the service account and the correctness of contact channels. Email is the first implemented step; phone confirmation follows as the next slice.
registerNext.auth.title: Authentication card
registerNext.authorization.copy: Authorization is granted only through group membership, evidence and scoped relationship to a person, company, vessel, client, task or control function.
registerNext.authorization.title: Authorization and visibility
registerNext.copy: CrewPortGlobal first confirms the person and service account, then collects the right cards for the work path and grants authorization only after evidence review.
registerNext.paths.employer.copy: For demand-side company, vessel, authority evidence and crew request data.
registerNext.paths.employer.title: Employer-side request card
registerNext.paths.seafarer.copy: For supply-side qualification, documents, availability, compensation expectations and matching readiness.
registerNext.paths.seafarer.title: Seafarer workforce card
registerNext.paths.title: Capability-card request
registerNext.person.copy: The public registration form creates or confirms the person/user record. It does not create a seafarer profile, company authority, vessel authority or operator access by itself.
registerNext.person.title: Physical person card
registerNext.tasks.email.copy: The service account email is confirmed for this browser session.
registerNext.tasks.email.title: Email confirmation
registerNext.tasks.path.copy: After account authentication, the user will request seafarer supply or employer-side demand cards.
registerNext.tasks.path.title: Choose the work path
registerNext.tasks.phone.copy: Phone verification should be configured in the next authentication slice.
registerNext.tasks.phone.title: Phone confirmation
registerNext.tasks.title: My tasks
registerNext.title: Continue step by step.
selected.copy: Each authorization form opens on its own page. Complete every selected form and attach the documents needed for evidence review.
selected.employer.copy: Demand-side authorization request with company, vessel, authority and buyer evidence.
selected.employer.title: Buyer / Employer form
selected.forms.empty: No forms are selected yet. Return to the authorization selection page.
selected.forms.title: Selected authorization forms
selected.seafarer.copy: Professional supply-side authorization request with qualification, availability and maritime document evidence.
selected.seafarer.title: Seafarer / Specialist form
selected.tasks.forms.copy: Fill each selected authorization form on its dedicated page. Upload fields currently save document metadata only until backend file storage is implemented.
selected.tasks.forms.title: Open selected forms
selected.tasks.review.copy: Submission for review, evidence validation and status granting are separate backend steps.
selected.tasks.review.title: Human review
selected.tasks.title: My tasks
selected.title: Complete the selected authorization forms.
sf.copy: This form collects the professional and document evidence needed to request seafarer or maritime specialist status. Saving the form does not publish the candidate or grant authorization automatically.
sf.docs.copy: Attach the documents that prove identity, qualification and readiness. Files are not uploaded to the server yet; metadata is saved locally until backend storage is implemented.
sf.docs.title: Documents for authorization review
sf.form.title: Professional status request
sf.note.placeholder: Short work history, certificates, restrictions, preferred routes
sf.status.saved: Seafarer / Specialist authorization form draft saved. No status is granted automatically.
sf.tasks.docs.copy: Upload controls are prepared for the future backend. In this slice the browser stores document metadata only.
sf.tasks.docs.title: Attach authorization documents
sf.tasks.form.copy: Provide rank, department, availability, expected conditions and supporting notes.
sf.tasks.form.title: Fill professional data
sf.tasks.title: My tasks
sf.title: Complete the seafarer / specialist form.
site.metaDescription: CrewPortGlobal process overview is now shown as a compact BP-015 operating cycle on the home page.
vacancies.empty.copy: When reviewed employer-side demand is ready for public display, this page will show a read-only summary of the listing.
vacancies.empty.title: No public vacancies are available yet
vacancies.filters.copy: The public page describes the publication rules and current reviewed availability.
vacancies.filters.country.placeholder: Broad public visitors do not receive private candidate or client data.
vacancies.filters.duration.option1: Applications and operational actions belong in authenticated work areas.
vacancies.filters.duration.option2: Tasks are assigned through scoped work queues.
vacancies.filters.duration.option3: The personal cabinet is built after authorization.
vacancies.filters.joinDate.placeholder: Candidates are recommended only after reviewed demand and supply alignment.
vacancies.filters.position.option1: Demand must be connected to a verified employer-side requester.
vacancies.filters.position.option2: Requested rank and department must be clear.
vacancies.filters.position.option3: The request must be linked to reviewed authority evidence.
vacancies.filters.position.option4: Public visitors see only approved summaries.
vacancies.filters.salary.option1: Rank, joining date, duration and compensation range must be ready for publication.
vacancies.filters.salary.option2: Compensation may be shown only when approved for public display.
vacancies.filters.title: The public page is read-only until a verified listing exists.
vacancies.filters.vessel.option1: Company and vessel context must be clear enough for review.
vacancies.filters.vessel.option2: Vessel type is part of demand matching.
vacancies.filters.vessel.option3: Private vessel details are not exposed by default.
vacancies.format.title: What each vacancy card will show
vacancies.hero.copy: This public page explains how vacancy visibility works. Operational request creation, candidate matching and applications happen only after registration, authentication and scoped authorization.
vacancies.hero.note: Public vacancies appear only when reviewed vacancy data is ready.
vacancies.hero.title: Reviewed maritime vacancies are shown only when demand data is ready.
vacancies.live.emptyFiltered: No reviewed vacancies are available for public display.
vacancies.live.title: Published maritime vacancies
vacancies.registered.boundary: Read-only preview excludes contacts, documents, private notes and candidate data.
vacancies.registered.empty: Registered crew request preview is not available yet.
vacancies.registered.error: Could not load registered request preview.
vacancies.registered.title: Safe preview from registered demand data
vacancies.side.boundary.copy: CrewPortGlobal does not imply automatic employment, automatic approval or automatic candidate presentation.
vacancies.side.boundary.title: Trust rule
vacancies.side.status.copy: Public vacancy data is read-only here. Service actions move to the authenticated personal cabinet.
vacancies.side.status.title: Current status
vacancies.trust.copy: The vacancies page does not publish fake jobs, fake employers or automatic employment claims. Public listings appear only when reviewed employer and vacancy information is ready for display.
vacancies.trust.title: CrewPortGlobal shows live vacancies only when reviewed data is ready.
verify.access.denied: Operator access or approved team session required.
verify.access.label: Operator access token
verify.access.teamHint: Approved team users can sign in through /team/ and return here; review_team / reviewer permissions are required for shortlist operations.
verify.action.deleteConfirm: Hide this vacancy request and require manager confirmation?
verify.action.deleteSaved: Deletion requested. Vacancy hidden from operator queue; manager confirmation required.
verify.action.noteRequired: Needs correction requires a review note.
verify.action.primaryHint: Review outcomes and secondary actions are inside the workspace.
verify.action.unavailable: This action is not available for the current operator role.
verify.candidateSearch.approvalError: Could not update internal shortlist approval.
verify.candidateSearch.copy: Read-only internal search. It does not create applications, change statuses or present candidates to employers.
verify.candidateSearch.createDraftDisabl: Run candidate search before creating an internal shortlist draft.
verify.candidateSearch.draftCreated: Internal shortlist draft created: {status}. Employer visible: {visible}.
verify.candidateSearch.draftError: Could not create internal shortlist draft.
verify.candidateSearch.empty: No candidate rows returned for this vacancy.
verify.candidateSearch.reviewApplication: Review applications created: {count}. Status: {status}. Employer visible: {visible}.
Approve the internal shortlist before creating review applications.
verify.candidateSearch.sideEffects: No side effects: no applications, no status changes, no employer visibility.
verify.card.selectRequired: Select a review target card first.
verify.col.name: Name
verify.completion.approveInternalShortli: Visible until the internal shortlist is approved, rejected or blocked by the approval guard.
verify.completion.companyVerification: Visible until employer authority, company context and linked demand context are reviewed or sent for correction.
verify.completion.createReviewApplicatio: Visible until candidate presentation review applications are created or blocked by guard.
verify.completion.createShortlist: Visible until an internal shortlist draft is created or the candidate-search guard blocks creation.
verify.completion.crewRequest: Visible until crew request completeness is reviewed and the next request-supply matching operation can be computed.
verify.completion.deletionConfirmation: Visible until manager confirms or rejects the deletion request.
verify.completion.genericReview: Visible until the responsible user records the required review outcome.
verify.completion.reviewCandidatePresent: Visible until candidate presentation is approved for employer-facing review or blocked by guard.
verify.completion.seafarerProfile: Visible until profile and source-card readiness are reviewed, corrected or blocked by the review guard.
verify.completion.vacancyApplication: Visible until candidate presentation review is approved, rejected or blocked by the approval guard.
verify.deletionTask.completed: Deletion decision recorded: {status}. Hidden from queue: {hidden}.
verify.deletionTask.copy: Confirm or reject the pending vacancy deletion request. Confirmation keeps the vacancy closed without physical deletion; rejection restores the previous workflow status.
verify.deletionTask.error: Could not record deletion confirmation decision.
verify.details.debugHidden: Technical debug payload is hidden in normal review mode.
verify.details.empty: Select a queue item to load full draft details.
verify.details.title: Review item details
verify.empty: Queue is empty. No submitted drafts yet.
verify.emptyFiltered: No items match the current filters in {lane}. Lane total: {laneCount}.
verify.error: Could not load queue. Please try again.
verify.history.title: Review history
verify.note.label: Review note (optional)
verify.note.placeholder: Add note for review action. Required for Needs correction.
verify.postAction.cardNext: Continue reviewing required source cards or return to Team tasks after blockers are resolved.
verify.postAction.deletionNext: Next computed task: manager deletion confirmation. Responsible group: owners.
verify.postAction.recompute: The task list is recomputed from the current object state; this task remains visible only while its visibility condition still applies.
verify.postAction.returnTeam: Return to Team tasks to see the recalculated queue.
verify.presentationTask.copy: Approve employer-facing presentation only after the backend approval guard confirms consent, source-card state and employer payload safety.
verify.shortlistTask.copy: Execute the selected computed operation. Backend access and guard checks remain the source of truth.
verify.shortlistTask.title: Task action
verify.shortlistTask.unavailable: Selected operation is not available for this draft: {items}
verify.task.notFound: Task target is not available in the current queue.
verify.title: Submitted Draft Queue
verify.visibilityReason.completed: Shown by the current filter as a control record; no primary execution remains.
verify.visibilityReason.computedOperatio: Visible until this computed operation is completed or blocked by guard.
verify.visibilityReason.correction: Visible until correction is submitted and the blocker is resolved.
verify.visibilityReason.inReview: Visible until the reviewer records the outcome.
verify.visibilityReason.managerConfirmat: Visible until manager confirms or rejects the deletion request.
verify.visibilityReason.submitted: Visible because submitted data requires a human review outcome.
verify.workspaceActions.copy: Use these actions only after reviewing the opened internal work item. Queue rows show one primary task; review outcomes and deletion requests are handled here.
